task:
Input: Patient-specific sequencing data (tumor DNA/RNA, healthy DNA), HLA typing results, mutation VAF, gene expression (mean/variance), and prediction scores for peptide cleavage, MHC binding, and pMHC stability (from tools like pVACtools); vaccine manufacturing budget (maximum number of neoantigen elements).
Output: Optimal personalized neoantigen vaccine composition (a set of neoantigen elements), quantitative vaccine efficacy metrics (per-cell immune response probability, coverage ratio of tumor cells, IoU of optimal vaccine compositions), and optimization runtime data.

data.name: cell-populations.csv
data.description: This file contains simulated cancer cell populations for multiple repetitions. Each row represents a peptide presented by a specific cell, including cell ID, presented peptide, HLA allele, simulation name (e.g., '100-cells.10x'), and mutation identifier.

data.name: final-response-likelihoods.csv
data.description: For each simulated cell, this file provides the final probability of immune response (p_response) and its log value, along with the number of presented peptides, population identifier, and vaccine type. Used for generating response probability distributions and coverage curves.

data.name: optimization_runtime_data.csv
data.description: Contains the optimization runtime (in seconds) for different cell population sizes (e.g., 100, 1000) for each patient sample (3812, 3942, ...). Used to generate Figure 6 (runtime vs population size).

data.name: selected-vaccine-elements.budget-10.minsum.adaptive.csv
data.description: Lists the selected vaccine elements (peptides/mutations) under the MinSum objective with a budget of 10. Includes peptide, repetition, simulation name, weight, and run time. Used for recall analysis and vaccine composition.

data.name: sim-specific-response-likelihoods.csv
data.description: Provides response probabilities for specific simulation repetitions. Similar to final-response-likelihoods but with more detailed simulation identifiers (including repetition).

data.name: vaccine-elements.scores.100-cells.10x.rep-0.csv
data.description: One of 10 replicate files (rep-0 to rep-9) containing cell-level scores for each vaccine element. Each row gives the response probability for a specific cell and vaccine element, along with log probabilities. Used to aggregate cell response probabilities.

data.name: vaccine-elements.scores.100-cells.10x.rep-1.csv
data.description: Replicate 1 of the vaccine element scores. Same structure as rep-0.

data.name: vaccine-elements.scores.100-cells.10x.rep-2.csv
data.description: Replicate 2 of the vaccine element scores.

data.name: vaccine-elements.scores.100-cells.10x.rep-3.csv
data.description: Replicate 3 of the vaccine element scores.

data.name: vaccine-elements.scores.100-cells.10x.rep-4.csv
data.description: Replicate 4 of the vaccine element scores.

data.name: vaccine-elements.scores.100-cells.10x.rep-5.csv
data.description: Replicate 5 of the vaccine element scores.

data.name: vaccine-elements.scores.100-cells.10x.rep-6.csv
data.description: Replicate 6 of the vaccine element scores.

data.name: vaccine-elements.scores.100-cells.10x.rep-7.csv
data.description: Replicate 7 of the vaccine element scores.

data.name: vaccine-elements.scores.100-cells.10x.rep-8.csv
data.description: Replicate 8 of the vaccine element scores.

data.name: vaccine-elements.scores.100-cells.10x.rep-9.csv
data.description: Replicate 9 of the vaccine element scores.

data.name: vaccine.budget-10.minsum.adaptive.csv
data.description: Simplified vaccine composition file listing selected peptides and their counts/weights. Used for recall analysis.
